title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud REST API - Security Posture and Alerts From Logic Apps
applies_to: Logic Apps pulling Defender for Cloud security recommendations, alerts, secure score, and assessments
Monthly security posture reporting workflows aggregating MDFC data across subscriptions
Security teams extracting MDFC data without a dedicated managed connector

confirmed_facts.fact: Defender for Cloud has no dedicated Logic Apps managed connector. All MDFC data must be pulled via the HTTP action using MSI auth with audience 'https://management.azure.com/'. This is the same ARM audience used for Sentinel REST calls.
confirmed_facts.source: Azure connector catalog — no Microsoft.Security connector exists; MDFC API is ARM-scoped

confirmed_facts.fact: List security alerts: GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{sub}/providers/Microsoft.Security/alerts?api-version=2022-01-01. Returns all active alerts. Filter by severity: $filter=properties/severity eq 'High'. Filter by status: $filter=properties/status eq 'Active'.

List security recommendations: GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{sub}/providers/Microsoft.Security/recommendations?api-version=2020-01-01. Returns all active recommendations. Each item has properties.displayName, properties.status.code (Healthy, Unhealthy, NotApplicable), properties.severity, and properties.remediationDescription.

Get subscription secure score: GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{sub}/providers/Microsoft.Security/secureScores/ascScore?api-version=2020-01-01. Returns properties.score.current (points earned) and properties.score.max (maximum possible). The percentage is current/max * 100.
confirmed_facts.source: MDFC REST API — Secure Scores - Get

confirmed_facts.fact: List all secure score controls: GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{sub}/providers/Microsoft.Security/secureScoreControls?api-version=2020-01-01. Each control has properties.displayName, properties.score.current, properties.score.max, properties.healthyResourceCount, properties.unhealthyResourceCount.
confirmed_facts.source: MDFC REST API — Secure Score Controls - List

confirmed_facts.fact: List assessments (resource-level health checks): GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{sub}/providers/Microsoft.Security/assessments?api-version=2020-01-01. Returns all assessments across all resources. Each item has properties.status.code and properties.resourceDetails.id (the affected resource ARM ID). Unhealthy assessment = failed check.

Assessments can also be queried at resource scope: GET https://management.azure.com/{resourceId}/providers/Microsoft.Security/assessments?api-version=2020-01-01. Substitute a full resource ARM ID (e.g., /subscriptions/{sub}/resourceGroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}) to scope results to one resource.
confirmed_facts.source: MDFC REST API — Assessments - List (resource scope)

confirmed_facts.fact: Secure score at management group scope: GET https://management.azure.com/providers/Microsoft.Management/managementGroups/{mgId}/providers/Microsoft.Security/secureScores/ascScore?api-version=2020-01-01. Requires Security Reader at the management group level. This is the correct endpoint for multi-subscription posture reporting.
confirmed_facts.source: MDFC REST API — Secure Scores - Get (management group scope)

confirmed_facts.fact: All MDFC REST calls paginate using nextLink. The Alerts list in particular can be large on active subscriptions — always implement pagination. The nextLink URL is ready to use as-is and already contains the api-version.
confirmed_facts.source: ARM pagination conventions applied to MDFC API

confirmed_facts.fact: Required RBAC role for read-only MDFC data pulls: Security Reader on the subscription (built-in role). This grants access to all Microsoft.Security/* read operations. Owner, Contributor, and Security Admin all also include read access. Reader (general) does NOT grant access to Microsoft.Security resources.
confirmed_facts.source: MDFC RBAC documentation — Security Reader vs general Reader distinction

confirmed_facts.fact: Alert dismissal/resolution: PATCH https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{sub}/providers/Microsoft.Security/alerts/{alertName}/dismiss?api-version=2022-01-01 with no body. To update status: PUT .../alerts/{alertName}?api-version=2022-01-01 with body { properties: { status: 'Dismissed' } }.
confirmed_facts.source: MDFC REST API — Alerts - Update State

confirmed_facts.fact: The alertName (resource name in the URL) is not the alert's displayName. It is the system-generated identifier in the 'name' field of the alert ARM resource, typically a GUID-like string. Use it for per-alert operations.

Defender for Cloud alerts have a remediationSteps array in properties. Each step is a string. For monthly reports, joining remediationSteps with a line break produces actionable content for the remediation column.

get_secure_score_action.description: Fetch the current secure score for a subscription

list_alerts_action.description: List all active High-severity MDFC alerts

list_recommendations_action.description: List all Unhealthy recommendations for monthly posture report

secure_score_controls_action.description: List all secure score controls with scores for breakdown reporting

monthly_posture_report_pattern.description: Sequence for a monthly MDFC posture report — runs all four pulls in order and assembles for SharePoint/email
monthly_posture_report_pattern.step_3_alerts: HTTP_List_MDFC_Alerts — GET alerts?$filter=severity eq High and status eq Active (with pagination)
monthly_posture_report_pattern.step_4_recommendations: HTTP_List_Recommendations — GET recommendations?$filter=status/code eq Unhealthy (with pagination)
monthly_posture_report_pattern.step_5_compose: Compose_Report_Payload — assemble all data into a single JSON object
monthly_posture_report_pattern.step_6_persist: Create SharePoint item or call Power BI push dataset
monthly_posture_report_pattern.note: Steps 1-4 can run in parallel (no dependencies between them) — omit runAfter between them to let Logic Apps execute concurrently. Only step_5 depends on all four completing.

implementation_notes: There is no MDFC managed connector for Logic Apps. The HTTP action with MSI is the only pattern. Auth audience is always 'https://management.azure.com/' (with trailing slash).
Security Reader is the minimum RBAC role. Assign it at subscription scope, not resource group scope — MDFC resources are subscription-level providers.
The Recommendations API can return thousands of items on large subscriptions. Use $filter=properties/status/code eq 'Unhealthy' to limit results to actionable items only.
The Alerts API uses api-version 2022-01-01. The older 2019-01-01 version is deprecated and returns a different schema — do not use it.

Secure score computation: current/max gives a ratio; multiply by 100 for percentage. Logic Apps expression: mul(div(float(current), float(max)), 100). Cast to float first to avoid integer division returning 0.
For multi-subscription reporting, run the secure score and alerts calls in a foreach loop over an array of subscription IDs. Collect results into a union array.
The $expand=definition query parameter on secureScoreControls returns additional metadata including the control's recommendations list and description. Include it for detailed control-level reports.
MDFC alerts have a startTimeUtc and endTimeUtc in properties. For monthly filters, use OData: $filter=properties/startTimeUtc ge 2025-03-01T00:00:00Z to scope to the reporting period.
remediationSteps is an array of strings, not a single string. Use join() to concatenate for CSV or text output: @join(item()?['properties']?['remediationSteps'], ' | ').

Assessments provide resource-level granularity — useful when you need to know exactly which VMs or storage accounts failed a specific check. They complement recommendations (which aggregate across resources).
common_failures: MSI missing Security Reader role — all MDFC calls return 403. General Reader role is NOT sufficient for Microsoft.Security resources.
Using general Reader RBAC role — returns 403 because Microsoft.Security/* requires Security Reader specifically.
Wrong api-version on alerts endpoint — using 2019-01-01 returns a different schema where alert properties are at different paths.
Integer division in secure score percentage — div(0, 100) returns 0 in Logic Apps integer math. Cast current and max to float before dividing.
Not paginating recommendations — silently gets only the first page (~100 items) on subscriptions with many resources.
Using displayName instead of name (GUID) as the alertName in per-alert REST operations — returns 404.
Querying at resource group scope for subscription-level resources — Microsoft.Security providers are subscription-scoped and return 404 when addressed through a resource group path.
